section_title: What types of amendment can be made under section 103 of the Companies Act 2016?
content:
Q: What types of amendment can be made under section 103 of the Companies Act 2016?

A: If a company has identified any error or omission in the Register of Member kept by the company, the person aggrieved may file an application to court to rectify the Register of Member.

section_title: Upon obtaining a court order under section 103 of the Companies Act 2016, how does the company notify SSM in order to do
content:
Q: Upon obtaining a court order under section 103 of the Companies Act 2016, how does the company notify SSM in order to do the necessary rectification?

A: If the rectification is made to the Register of Member which is kept by the company, there is no requirement to notify SSM unless the error also appeared in the register kept by SSM. In such case the company must submit the court order together with amended register under section 51 of the Companies Act 2016 to SSM.

section_title: Can the amendments to "amount of premium paid (RM)" be made in more than 2 decimal points? Example Form 24 dated 9.1.201
content:
Q: Can the amendments to "amount of premium paid (RM)" be made in more than 2 decimal points? Example Form 24 dated 9.1.2015 amended from RM1.64 to RM1.644503211.

A: It should be limited to 4 decimal points only.

section_title: If a company amends its constitution, do they have to submit to SSM the amended constitution?
content:
Q: If a company amends its constitution, do they have to submit to SSM the amended constitution?

A: Yes, the company has to submit the amended constitution with SSM as amendment is not rectification. Any new amendments to the constitution will replace the earlier submitted constitution.
